overview: Boros Burn is a fast, aggressive deck that aims to reduce the opponent's life total to zero using a suite of efficient one-mana red spells and white support. The deck leverages signature cards like Boltwave and Boros Charm to apply relentless pressure while maintaining board control. Screaming Nemesis provides a flexible finisher or removal option that scales with the number of creatures in play. Success relies on maximizing early damage output and disrupting opponent development before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Dealing direct damage to the opponent's life total with one-mana red spells
Using Boros Charm to clear blockers or finish the game
Deploying Screaming Nemesis as a high-impact threat or removal

keep_criteria: Hands with multiple one-mana red spells
At least two lands for turns one and two
Keep any hand with a land and a one-mana spell (Shock, Lightning Helix, Boros Charm, or a 1/1 like Raging Goblin/Firebrand Archer).
Keep two lands plus one mana spell, or two lands plus two one-mana spells.
Prioritize hands that can play a turn-one or turn-two threat or spell.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only white cards or high-cost threats
Hands with three or more lands
Mulligan hands with three or more lands, no one-mana spells, or only expensive threats (Kari Zev, Grim Lavamander, Screaming Nemesis) without early damage.

early_game: Play lands and cast one-mana red spells for direct damage
Use Boros Charm for damage or early board control
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Shock/Lightning Helix at the opponent or a 1/1 creature.
If holding a 1/1, play land, cast 1/1, end.
Turn 2: Play second land, cast another 1/1 or cheap burn, attack with everything.
If drawing Screaming Nemesis, cast it on turn 2 or 3 to capitalize on instant/sorcery spells, then use Boros Charm to pump it into a lethal 3/1 or remove a blocker.
Mana base: Prioritize dual lands (Steam Vents, Bloodstained Mire, Stomping Ground) early
mid_game: Deploy Screaming Nemesis to pressure life total or remove key blockers
Cast Boltwave to clear the board and deal residual damage
Maintain aggressive posture while answering threats
conserve lands but never miss land drops.
Use direct damage to close out games or remove key blockers.
Don't overcommit to the board if you can win with spells.
Prioritize keeping mana open for Boros Charm and Shock to maximize efficiency.
late_game: Use remaining red spells to close out the game
Leverage Boros Charm's combat trick mode if needed
Push damage every turn until the opponent folds or wins
Kari Zev, Swiftblade Scavenger, Kuldotha Cackler, Grim Lavamander, and Screaming Nemesis are your primary win conditions.
Chain direct damage spells with combat to reach 20.
Boros Charm can pump a threat into lethal or remove a blocker to enable a kill.
Go all-in when you have lethal damage in hand/creature pool, or when the opponent has no removal/sweepers.
Use Boros Charm to pump a threat into lethal or remove a blocker.
If the opponent is at 3 or less life, attack with everything and burn the rest.

key_cards.name: Boltwave
key_cards.role: Board wipe and direct damage spell
key_cards.notes: Cast when the opponent has multiple creatures to maximize value, or use early to clear a single blocker and deal two damage to the player.

key_cards.name: Boros Charm
key_cards.role: Flexible one-mana spell for damage, removal, or combat tricks
key_cards.notes: Choose damage mode on turn one, removal mode to clear blockers, or combat trick mode to swing for lethal or protect your attackers.

key_cards.name: Screaming Nemesis
key_cards.role: High-impact creature threat and conditional removal
key_cards.notes: Play as a finisher when the board is clear, or use its enter-the-battlefield effect to remove key opponent creatures and apply pressure simultaneously.

sequencing_tips: Cast direct damage spells before playing creatures to avoid giving the opponent free blockers
Hold Boros Charm in hand to respond to opponent's combat tricks or removal
Time Boltwave to hit multiple creatures while leaving enough damage to close the game
Use Screaming Nemesis's ETB effect to clear the path for your own attackers
Boltwave appears to be a typo or custom card
assuming it refers to a high-damage burn spell like Lightning Strike or Shock.
Always calculate exact damage to the opponent versus blockers.
Boros Charm's 'deal 2 to target creature' can remove key creatures, but prioritize life gain removal or pump in aggressive matchups.
Screaming Nemesis synergizes heavily with your instant/sorcery suite
cast it early to maximize its +1/+1 trigger.

matchups.advice: Prioritize direct damage over board presence and use Boros Charm to remove key threats like Kari Zev.
Race quickly before their combo pieces assemble and use Boltwave to clear their early board.
Apply early pressure to prevent them from stabilizing with removal and card draw.

Use Boros Charm and Shock on turn 1 to pressure before counters are up. Save Lightning Axe/Dragon's Rage Channeler for later when you can value out counters. Attack with creatures to force counter decisions and protect your spells with cheap threats.
Outpace them. Play lands, cast burn, attack. Don't trade creatures; burn their ramp sources or the opponent directly. Use Boros Charm to pump your threats into lethal before they can stabilize.
Use Boros Charm to pump your 1/1s into lethal. Use Shock/Lightning Helix to remove key threats. Don't overextend; race them. Track their life total and your own to ensure you win the race.

Pressure early, then pivot to value engines (Dragon's Rage Channeler, Lightning Axe) if they stabilize. Use Red Elemental Blast to counter their key counterspells. Attack with creatures to force them to spend removal on threats rather than your spells.

It is a race. Prioritize lethal damage over board presence. Use Boros Charm to pump your threat into lethal or remove theirs. Track life totals carefully; often the first to deal 20 wins. Save removal for their threats and burn for the opponent.

common_threats: Watch for life gain (Orzhov), sweepers (Selesnya/Golgari), counterspells (Izzet/Niv), and graveyard recursion (Golgari).
Key tells include the opponent tapping for multiple colors, holding up mana past turn 3, or playing enchantments/planeswalkers.
Adjust your damage sequencing to bypass life gain or remove key threats before attacking.
